title: Breitmann in Rome
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dereן¿½s lighds oopon de Appian,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey shine de road entlang;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und from ein hundert tombs dere brumms
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A wild Lateinisch song;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ It rings from Neroן¿½s goldnen haus;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Evoe! here he coom!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Fly oud, ye mן¿½nads, from your craves!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Hans Breitmannן¿½s got to Rome!

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For vhile de lamp holts oud to purn,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Or von goot shpark ish dere,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dereן¿½s hope for all of dem whose lives
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ish doun in Lempri?re.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Von real, shenuine heathen
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Is coom at last to home;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ye shleepin gotts, lift oop your hets
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Hans Breitmann lifes in Rome!

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Silenus mit der Hercules,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dere-to der Maiaן¿½s sohn,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ish all unite in Breitmann
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To make a stunnin one.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Frau Venus mit de Bacchanals
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ist shmile to see him come;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De Vesta only toorn her pack
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhen Breitmann kit to Rome.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He vented to de Vacuum,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhere de Bope ish keep his bulls;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Boot couldnן¿½t vind dem, dough he heardt
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dat all de blace vas fools.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dere ish here and dere some ochsen,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Right manivest I see;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Boot de bools all comes from Irish priests,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Said Breitemann, said he.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und goinן¿½ py de Vacuum,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und passinן¿½ troo de yard;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mein Gott! how vas he stoomple, vhen
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He see der Schweitzer guard,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mit efery kinds of colors tresst,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Like shtreamers in de van.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Hans Wurst ist stets ein Deutscher gן¿½west,ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Das marked der Breitemann.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und dus replied an guartsmann:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½I shoys to see you here:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ich bin dem Bapst sei Laibgaertner.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dazu a halberthier.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dis purpur kleid of yellow-plue
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vas made, ash I hafe heard,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Py von Hans Michel Angelo,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der tailor of our guard.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Veן¿½re shoost von hoondert dirty strong,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ve list for twenty year;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De serfice ist not pad, boot dis
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Verdamm das Rן¿½misch bier!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For ven mit birra gazzosa
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A maiden fills my glass,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ She might ash vell gife gift ash say
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Feinslieb, ich schenk dir dass!ן¿½ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und dus rebly der Breitmann:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Un Tedesco Italianazato,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ein Deutscher toorned Italian, ish
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Il diavolo in carnato.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Your clothes are like infernal flames,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey burn my fery soul;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Boot to-night weן¿½ll trink togedder nun
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Liebן¿½landsmann lebe wohl!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ At de Sherman artisdsן¿½ festa,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhere all vas pright und fair,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Tvas fairer und more prighterfull
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhen Breitmann enter dere.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und der vaiters in de Greco
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ (So long he trinked und sot)
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vas called him Lן¿½Ubbriacone
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Tvas de name der Breitmann got.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He saw a veller in de shtreet,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vot sell some friction-matches;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De kind dey call Infallible,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For dey blazes ven you scratches.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey dragged him off to brison,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und tied him mit a rope;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For in Rome dereן¿½s nix Infallible,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey said, excebt de Bope.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Hans see de crate Prometheus,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In Corsiniן¿½s gallery hang;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He tought apout de matches,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und it made his heart go bang.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Itן¿½s risk to carry light apout,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Too cheap for efery man;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ How de Lucifers is fallen!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ita dixit Breitmann.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He got among de Bopeן¿½s Zouaves,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey trinked from morn to night;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Den frolicked colle belle
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ontil de shky crew pright.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ It blease der Breitmann vonderfool,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And dus he often say:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Zouaviter in modo ish
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der real Roman way.ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Boot oh, his heart burned vild mit fire,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ His eyes gefilled mit tears,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ At de gotts in efery bilder saal,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mit goatsן¿½ legs, tails, und ears.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und he sopped ן¿½Ach liebes Deutschland,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Bist here on every hand?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Was machst du Mephistophelן¿½s
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ So weit im Wן¿½lschen Land?ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Boot de wood-nymphs boorst out laughin,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der Garten-gott dere to,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und sait ן¿½Oldt Hans! vile youן¿½re apout
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ve nefer can look blue.ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Den Pan blay on his Syrinx,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To de tune of Mary Blane,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Donן¿½t gry pecause veן¿½re out of town,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Veן¿½re coming pack again.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Von day you got de yolk und vhite,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De next day only shells;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Von day dey holts a council,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und de next day ן¿½someding else!ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Idן¿½s bopes und kings, und gotts and dings,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Oopon dis eartly ball;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Boot for me idן¿½s all von frolic,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und a high oldt carnival!

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Rise oop, dou Odin-trafeler,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und toorn dee to de Nort,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Wherefrom, as Bible dells dee,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Crate efil shall come fort.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dere is mutterins in Ravenna,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und ere long dereן¿½ll come a turn,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A real hell-bender from de land
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Of Dieterich von Bern.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Und ven der Breitmannן¿½s prototype,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der Fictoor Manuel,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Cooms tromplin, tromplin troo de fern,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To give dis coontry hell.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und ven in La Comarca,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der is shtorm all in de air,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dy Gotts vill gife dee vork, mein Sohn,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Hans Breitmann shall be dere!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For a yar will nod be ofer
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Pefore de Frן¿½ntsch will run,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und de game at last be ented,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und Italy pe won.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und denn in roarin battle,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For hishtory so grand,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dy bannerן¿½ll lead de Uhlan spears,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ All in de Frankenland.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Charles G. Leland represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Charles G. Leland's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
